marketFocus: US-Iran ceasefire talks in a 'knife edge' state. AP reported both sides gave an 'in principle agreement' to extend the ceasefire for at least two weeks, but both US and Iranian officials pushed back on the reports. Trump said 'it could end either way' and is 'not thinking about extending the ceasefire.' Pakistan's army chief is heading to the US on Friday as part of mediation efforts. SPX +0.80% to a fresh record high at 7,023. NDX +1.40% at 26,205. Nikkei 225 rallied to a fresh record high above 59,000.

AUD Employment Change missed expectations but the Unemployment Rate held at 4.3% — the jobs increase was solely driven by a jump in full-time jobs. ASX 200 bucked the trend, giving back initial gains as losses in energy, resources, materials, financials and miners offset tech strength. DXY was slightly firmer with AUD outperforming and JPY lagging per the PMT London Session Recap. Brent settled at $94.93/bbl, WTI at $91.29/bbl — crude rangebound amid conflicting ceasefire signals.

Today's key events: UK GDP m/m (Feb) at 08:00 FFT — key event for GBP. ECB Minutes (Mar), SNB Minutes (Mar), ECB Schnabel/Nagel/Lane speak. Fed Williams and Miran speak. Earnings: TSMC, Abbott, Charles Schwab, PepsiCo, Netflix, Tesco. AUD Employment already released at 03:30 FFT (mixed result). No further red folder events today.

USD.tone: DXY Slightly Firmer but Lacking Direction — Fed On Hold; Trump Threatens Powell Firing
USD.summary: USD is Neutral today. DXY traded with a mild positive bias but 'lacked firm direction' per the PMT reports, remaining supported above the 98.00 mark without generating a clear directional signal. Fed's Musalem said the current rate range is 'likely appropriate for some time' and that the oil shock is likely feeding core inflation toward 3% through year-end — a hawkish hold signal that caps USD downside. Fed's Hammack said rates are 'in a good place.' US TIC flows were strongly positive (+$184.5bln vs prev -$38.4bln) — a structural positive for USD. However, Trump's threat to fire Fed Chair Powell if he doesn't leave introduces institutional uncertainty. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Neutral = ❌ no alignment.
drivers: DXY slightly firmer but lacked direction — supported above 98.00 without clear catalyst
Fed Musalem: rates 'appropriate for some time', oil shock feeding core inflation toward 3%
US TIC flows +$184.5bln vs prev -$38.4bln — strong capital inflows positive for USD
Trump threatens to fire Powell — institutional uncertainty caps USD upside
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Neutral = no alignment

EUR.tone: EUR/USD Back at 1.1800 — ECB June Hike Bets Build; EU Industrial Production Beat
EUR.summary: EUR is Bullish today. EUR/USD eked mild gains after returning to the 1.1800 handle. ECB's Kazaks said 'nothing against bets on two hikes starting in June' and that 'will see if April is too soon for action' — the market is now pricing June as the more likely first hike date. ECB's Muller said 'rate move at April meeting still cannot be ruled out' and ECB's Demarco warned an 'adverse scenario could require two rate hikes.' EU Industrial Production MoM (Feb) beat at 0.4% vs 0.3% expected. ECB Minutes (Mar) are due today — watch for any hawkish signals. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: EUR/USD back at 1.1800 — mild gains as ECB June hike bets build
ECB Kazaks: 'nothing against bets on two hikes starting June' — June hike now base case
ECB Muller: 'April rate move cannot be ruled out' — keeps near-term hawkish optionality
EU Industrial Production MoM (Feb) 0.4% vs 0.3% exp — beat supports EUR
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ALIGNED

GBP.tone: GBP Edging Higher — UK GDP (Feb) at 08:00 FFT Key Event; BoE Greene Speaks
GBP.summary: GBP is Bullish today. GBP/USD gradually edged higher but remained contained within the 1.3500 handle as participants look ahead to UK GDP (Feb), Industrial and Manufacturing Production data at 08:00 FFT. The BoE hawkish hold narrative remains intact — BoE's Mann's comments on wage inflation expectations from Wednesday continue to support GBP. UK Chancellor Reeves is planning emergency energy relief for energy-intensive firms, and confirmed UK participation in the EU's EUR 90bln loan to Ukraine — both positive for UK fiscal credibility. Multiple trading desks are recommending EUR/GBP longs (ING, Rabo, JPM) — watch for EUR/GBP upside pressure. BoE's Greene speaks today. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: UK GDP m/m (Feb) at 08:00 FFT — key event; beat would extend GBP strength
BoE hawkish hold narrative intact — Mann's wage inflation concern from Wednesday still in play
UK confirmed EUR 90bln Ukraine loan participation — fiscal credibility positive
Multiple desks recommend EUR/GBP longs (ING, Rabo, JPM) — watch for EUR/GBP upside
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = ALIGNED

JPY.tone: JPY Lagged in Session — IMF Dovish on BoJ; FinMin Cautious on Rate Hikes
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today. The yen lagged in the London session per the PMT London Session Recap — USD/JPY continued its pullback from the 159.00 territory before rebounding off lows, suggesting the yen's recent strength is running out of steam. The IMF said the BoJ can 'see through inflationary pressures from the Middle East conflict as second-round effects will be moderate' — a dovish signal that removes urgency for BoJ hikes. Japanese Finance Minister Katayama said 'raising interest rates could have a negative impact on the economy' — cautious on hikes. Japan's FX diplomat Minmura coordinated with US Treasury Secretary Bessent on FX, agreeing to 'stay in close contact' — verbal intervention risk caps excessive JPY weakness but does not change the directional bias. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: JPY lagged in London session — USD/JPY rebounded off lows, yen strength running out of steam
IMF: BoJ can 'see through' Middle East inflation pressures — removes urgency for hikes
FinMin Katayama: 'raising rates could have negative impact on economy' — cautious on hikes
Japan FX diplomat Minmura coordinating with Bessent on FX — verbal intervention risk
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = ALIGNED

AUD.tone: AUD Employment MISSED — ASX Underperformed; Refinery Fire Adds Uncertainty
AUD.summary: AUD is Neutral today. The AUD Employment Change missed expectations — the jobs increase was solely driven by a jump in full-time jobs, with the Unemployment Rate unchanged at 4.3%. While the full-time composition is positive, the headline miss removes the clean bullish catalyst. ASX 200 bucked the positive risk-on trend, giving back initial gains as losses in energy, resources, materials, financials and miners dominated. A fire at Viva Energy's refinery may hit petrol production — a domestic supply disruption that adds uncertainty. Australia secured 100mln litres extra diesel from Brunei and South Korea — a positive energy security measure. AUD 'remained afloat' per the FX section but without a clear directional driver. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = ❌ no alignment.
drivers: AUD Employment Change missed expectations — full-time jobs jump saved headline, but mixed
Unemployment Rate unchanged at 4.3% — no deterioration but no improvement
ASX 200 underperformed — losses in energy, resources, materials, financials offset tech
Viva Energy refinery fire may hit petrol production — domestic supply disruption
Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = no alignment

CAD.tone: Oil Rangebound — Ceasefire Extension 'In Principle' Caps Upside; Energy Sector Lagged
CAD.summary: CAD is Bearish today. Crude futures were 'little changed' following the prior day's near-5% drop, with Brent settling at $94.93/bbl and WTI at $91.29/bbl. The 'in principle' agreement to extend the ceasefire caps oil upside — markets are pricing in a deal, which removes the geopolitical risk premium from crude. The energy sector was again the clear laggard in equity markets. The IEA's demand forecast cut from Wednesday continues to weigh on the medium-term oil outlook. White House is expected to urge oil and gas companies to increase drilling — a bearish supply signal for oil. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: Brent $94.93/bbl, WTI $91.29/bbl — rangebound after prior day's 5% drop
Ceasefire extension 'in principle' caps oil upside — geopolitical risk premium fading
Energy sector again the clear laggard in equity markets
White House to urge oil/gas companies to increase drilling — bearish supply signal
Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = ALIGNED

CHF.tone: SNB Surcharge Cut Mildly Dovish — SNB Minutes Due Today; No Directional Catalyst
CHF.summary: CHF is Neutral today. The SNB lowered the special-rate surcharge from 50bps to 25bps effective July 1st — a mildly dovish signal that slightly reduces the cost of holding CHF reserves, but is insufficient to generate a directional bias. SNB Minutes (Mar) are due today — watch for any signals on the SNB's reaction function to the Middle East conflict and inflation outlook. The risk-on environment continues to reduce haven demand for CHF. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = no directional alignment.
drivers: SNB lowered special-rate surcharge from 50bps to 25bps (July 1) — mildly dovish signal
SNB Minutes (Mar) due today — watch for reaction function to Middle East/inflation
Risk-on environment reduces haven demand for CHF
No tier-1 data or strong directional catalyst for CHF today
Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = no directional alignment

NZD.tone: NZD RBNZ Repricing Intact — Antipodeans Afloat; NZD Food Inflation (Mar) Due
NZD.summary: NZD is Bullish today. The RBNZ hawkish repricing story from ANZ (hikes Jul/Sep/Oct) remains fully intact with no negative catalyst to challenge it. Antipodeans 'remained afloat' per the FX section despite the mixed AUD employment data — NZD held its ground. NZD Food Inflation (Mar) is due today — a beat would reinforce the RBNZ hawkish narrative. The combination of the RBNZ rate hike repricing and the broadly risk-on environment continues to provide a dual tailwind for NZD. Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = ✅ ALIGNED.
drivers: RBNZ hike repricing (Jul/Sep/Oct) intact — ANZ hawkish call unchallenged
Antipodeans 'remained afloat' despite mixed AUD employment — NZD held ground
NZD Food Inflation (Mar) due today — beat would reinforce RBNZ hawkish narrative
Risk-on environment provides tailwind alongside RBNZ story
Weekly Bullish + Daily Bullish = ALIGNED
